examples.title: Kinetic Energy Calculator
examples.description: Calculate kinetic energy given mass and velocity
examples.explanation: The formula KE = ½mv² is fundamental. For the car: KE = 0.5 × 1000 × 400 = 200,000 J. This demonstrates conversion of motion to energy.
examples.code:
FUNCTION calculate_kinetic_energy(mass, velocity):
 // mass in kg, velocity in m/s
 kinetic_energy = 0.5 * mass * (velocity ^ 2)
 RETURN kinetic_energy // in Joules

// Example: Car of 1000kg moving at 20 m/s
mass = 1000
velocity = 20
ke = calculate_kinetic_energy(mass, velocity)
PRINT "Kinetic Energy:", ke, "Joules"

examples.title: Energy Efficiency Calculator
examples.description: Calculate efficiency percentage of an energy conversion system
examples.explanation: Efficiency shows how well energy is converted. Power plants average 35-45%, showing significant energy losses as waste heat.
examples.code:
FUNCTION calculate_efficiency(input_energy, output_energy):
 // Both energies in Joules
 IF input_energy <= 0:
 PRINT "Error: Input energy must be positive"
 RETURN -1
 
 efficiency = (output_energy / input_energy) * 100
 
 // Determine efficiency category
 IF efficiency >= 80:
 rating = "Excellent"
 ELSE IF efficiency >= 50:
 rating = "Good"
 ELSE IF efficiency >= 30:
 rating = "Moderate"
 ELSE:
 rating = "Poor"
 
 RETURN efficiency, rating

// Example: Thermal power plant
input_energy = 100000 // 100 MJ from coal
output_energy = 35000 // 35 MJ electricity
efficiency, rating = calculate_efficiency(input_energy, output_energy)
PRINT "Efficiency:", efficiency, "% - Rating:", rating

examples.title: Potential Energy Calculator
examples.description: Calculate gravitational potential energy at height
examples.explanation: Potential energy at height is mgh. This water stored in dams is used in hydroelectric power plants to generate electricity through turbines.
